objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 roster authorization assessment with cross-tenant read exposure.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Roster data and teacher identities are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map the roster API authorization boundary.
actions: Load the synthetic roster API route behavior
Identify the teacher/class ownership fields used by the endpoint
successCriteria: Roster endpoint evidence is recorded with a durable evidence ID
noSubagents: authorization scope and data-handling decisions stay with the primary operator

actions: Replay a teacher-scoped request against another school's class roster
Record the exposed fields and expected ownership check
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites the request evidence and control-gap evidence
noSubagents: final severity and disclosure language stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic cross-tenant roster request
evidence.summary: A teacher-scoped request for another school's class returned student roster records.

evidence.title: Synthetic roster ownership check gap
evidence.summary: The synthetic route validates authentication but does not enforce class ownership against the requesting teacher.
evidence.content: Expected control: require class.schoolId and class.teacherId to match the authenticated teacher context before returning roster rows.

findings.title: Cross-tenant roster read exposure
findings.description: The synthetic roster endpoint returns student records for a class outside the requesting teacher's assigned school context.
findings.impact: A compromised or curious teacher account could read student roster data beyond its intended class or school boundary.
findings.remediation: Enforce server-side school and class ownership checks before returning roster rows, and add audit alerts for cross-school roster access attempts.
